votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the substring, prefix, and helper functions, and accurately translates the Coq specification into an executable boolean check.
The Python code correctly implements the substring, prefix, and helper functions, and accurately translates the Coq specification into a boolean check.
The Python code accurately translates the Coq string operations and list comprehensions, preserving the logic of the helper and specification functions.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification. The substring function uses Python slicing which matches the Coq behavior. The prefix function calls substring with start=0. The all_prefixes_helper builds a list of prefixes from length 1 to n, matching the Coq recursive definition that appends [prefix s n] at each step. The all_prefixes_spec checks the same three conditions: result equals all_prefixes_helper(s, len(s)), length of result equals length of s, and each element at index i equals prefix(s, i+1). All concrete behaviors match.
